item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually have. I honestly would love to see how both Cain and Able would react to seeing their little brother and what he has become.

Cain would probably feel great pity and sadness for Seth and feel guilty for what he’s done in the past since he no doubt changed with the countless millenia he’s lived thanks to the curse his father placed on him.

Able on the other hand…it’s very likely he WOULD recognize Seth at first, but still try to attack him and probably forcefully demand that Seth tells him where The Crown of Apollyon is.

item.title: Re: The Flying City
item.content:encoded:
"I dunno if this is the sort of thing that people even want to read anymore, but I do so I'm writing it."
I definitely do!
Very much looking forward to seeing where all of this is going.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This series is by far my favorite, this is a really good followup to SCP-4812, the kaktusverse is amazing +1

Also can someone provide a list of all SCPs referenced in this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the envoy from Saturn

Is this a reference to 2845? Anyway, its all great and the references are well done.
Cant wait to see the next installment in the kaktusverse. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great article, I really love the world building and biblical themes. +1

I only have 1 nitpick, the sentence :

In the center of the tree is a large, red organ is visible through openings in the bark.

It seemes like you need to remove one of the two "is" or add the word "which" before the second "is"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Correct me if I'm wrong, but I belive the other 3 profanities are the three entities in SCP-4812 "Wrath".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Considering what this city is and how big is its importance…
I would not be surprised if they moved Able and Cain back home to defend it.

+1, really enjoyed the story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I absolutely love this story line, this and SCP-4812 are my favorite SCPs, please do continue it.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Has anyone thought of “reintroducing” either Cain or Able to Seth? Cause that sound like quite a story

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Huh, I wonder who wrote this?"

It's Djkaktus

It's always Djkaktus

I swear this guy writes half the wiki.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Outstanding. I hope we see the other 3 profanities mentioned show up as SCPs of their own tied back to this one. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is a whole load to take in! ill have to read it a few times to fully grasp it all, even with my obsessive knowledge of useless scp lore. the thing that probably sparked my curiosity was the image of 4840-B, i instantly recognized the similarities with it and 2254 and that got me interested. this was very much a trip and i'm interested to see where this story goes! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is fantastic, and maybe my favorite piece of yours…

and he carried a lance they say could kill gods.

nice, was wondering when this spear would be referenced again.

The way this ties so many disparate ideas together into one cosmology… I feel like this could be another 001 proposal. It’s fantastic.

SCP-4840-A is also the only individual capable of entering the sealed structure beneath SCP-4840's great hall, which SCP-4840-B calls "the Old Library".

Tiny nitpick… not sure if this is the typo because I thought -B is effectively dead (or at least non communicative)

item.title: Re: Provisional Licensing Information
item.content:encoded: Notice that the rest of the images have now been sourced (they are present in the info panel).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very, very reluctant downvote. I like what you are doing with the worldbuilding but it does follow a pattern which I think undermines the piece as a whole; you have an excellent set-up prior to the interview, but the interview itself is a long narration straightforwardly explaining the vast majority of the mystery, without any real thematic or emotional arc binding it all together. It's certainly very well-written and I like a lot of the stuff you're doing in terms of worldbuilding, but the structure of this just doesn't quite click for me.

item.title: Provisional Licensing Information
item.content:encoded:
• "sonofson.jpg" is from Max Pixel, CC0 Public Domain.
  • "temple1.png" is from Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 3.0, taken by Steve F-E-Cameron.
  • "temple2.png" is from Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 3.0, taken by Steve F-E-Cameron.
  • "temple3.png" is from Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 3.0, taken by Rolf Süßbrich.
  • I couldn't find the sources of all other images so far.

item.title: Absolutely fantastic
item.content:encoded: Descriptive imagery that made you feel the scope of the city, the connections to existing lore, insight into early history, and phenomenal ending with fridge implications. The kaktus does it again +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Damn. This article was unnervingly good. I didn't even realize that I had been reading this article for 35 minutes. +2

EDIT: +1. I wish I could have given +2.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I get a feeling that YASH is written in all caps for a reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm. If YASH ate the crown, than what's in the 'Apollyon's Crown' lockbox in the Department of Abnormalities? Is if it is the crown, does that imply some deep connection between YASH and the Foundation?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Going by your lore, does 3000 ties back to 2747?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There is a lot going on here and much of it is amazing. It almost stretches credibility, however. If it were not for the fact that other similar OP SCPs exist I wouldn't be able to give it a +1, but given that it ties other SCP's together so nicely (and the fact that I just plain like it) I am going to do so.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
D E E P E S T L O R E

But seriously, as a foreverDM and a writer, I can appreciate good worldbuilding. And this is a gold medalist in that. Tying things together in a way that makes you say not just "Oh, that makes sense!", but "Was it like this all along?" Everything wraps together wonderfully, with a tangible history and effect on the other parts.

You're an inspiration to us all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a beautiful story but I am having trouble eating it as an SCP. I hope you don't begrudge a no-vote for the time being. I'll continue to digest what I've read and reflect. You've definitely succeeded in giving me things to think about and leave your article with.

item.title: Re: The Flying City
item.content:encoded: yet again you manage to write something so intersting and deep. +1. please continue really good stuff like this!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And so as the kaktuskanon expands, so does the L O R E.

Seriously though, I got goosebumps near the end of that interview log and I've never been more excited to see what you do next.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because "diluvian" means "related to floods", it doesn't reference a period of time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because antediluvian references the time between the beginning of Genesis and the Great Flood. Calling it "pre-antediluvian" would then mean the time before Genesis, which Seth references at the beginning of his conversation. I don't see why that wouldn't make sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's further evidence for "prediluvian" being correct, as surely the Foundation is not referring to "the time during and only during the Flood", but rather the time before that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If they cancel each other out, wouldn't it be diluvian?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
pre-antediluvian

Why not just say "prediluvian"? This construction makes no sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent worldbuilding as usual

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the wrath expanded cinematic universe

wrath: infinity apollyon when

item.title: The Flying City
item.content:encoded:
I've been working on this for a while. There's a lot going on here, and a lot more to come. If you want to read the first few entries in this series, read 2254 and 4812.

Thanks to Jekeled and Rounderhouse for reading this ahead of time. I dunno if this is the sort of thing that people even want to read anymore, but I do so I'm writing it.

Thanks in advance for your support.
